symptoms.symptom_name: Missed scheduled maintenance B12 (hydroxocobalamin) injections for ~6 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In a B12-dependent patient, an interrupted replacement schedule is the direct mechanism of recurrence.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constant, progressively worsening bilateral foot paraesthesia and numbness (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pernicious anaemia relapse with peripheral neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical distal sensory neuropathy is the characteristic neurological expression of B12 deficiency.
Bilateral, symmetrical, chronic-progressive sensory change is not a focal vascular pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical reduction in plantar light-touch sensation on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pernicious anaemia relapse with peripheral neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective distal sensory loss corroborates the B12 neuropathy — a clinician examination finding, not obtainable by the AI over chat; the AI infers neuropathy from the reported symptom only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound fatigue, lightheadedness and self-reported pallor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional symptoms of anaemia; supportive but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor with mild resting tachycardia (HR ~95) and stable blood pressure on clinician vitals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia and pallor of anaemia — clinician-measured; sealed, as the AI cannot obtain vitals without a device. Stable numbers argue against acute decompensation requiring emergency transport.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood count / blood film showing macrocytic (megaloblastic) anaemia and low serum B12

investigation_details.investigation_name: FBC, blood film, serum vitamin B12 and folate
investigation_details.expected_result: Macrocytic anaemia with low serum B12, consistent with relapse; used to confirm severity and exclude a second cause of anaemia.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of anaemia and any coexisting process go unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the megaloblastic pattern and low B12 — a laboratory test the AI cannot obtain in telehealth in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe agoraphobia limiting attendance for scheduled care

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Recurrent medical neglect of essential maintenance treatment
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The access barrier is the mechanism of the missed dosing and predicts recurrence unless care is brought to the patient.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, no dyspnoea, no focal/one-sided weakness, no loss of leg movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of ischaemic features makes the tachycardia compensatory rather than cardiac.
Preserved power and absence of focal deficit argue against an acute neurological emergency at this moment.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Missed replacement plus recurring fatigue plus progressive symmetrical neuropathy together identify the relapse and its urgency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBC, blood film, serum vitamin B12 and folate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the megaloblastic anaemia and low B12, quantifies severity, and screens for a coexisting cause — to be arranged with the in-person visit.
Baseline haematinics to interpret the anaemia and monitor response to replacement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat/serial home or clinic neurological screening for spread
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To detect ascending or worsening neuropathy that would raise urgency; formal neuro exam is in-person.
